# Transaction Lifecycle

## Docs

- [API](https://docs.rain.xyz/changelog/api.md)
- [Smart Contracts](https://docs.rain.xyz/changelog/contracts.md)
- [3ds.notification](https://docs.rain.xyz/changelog/webhooks/v1/3ds/notification.md)
- [card.notification](https://docs.rain.xyz/changelog/webhooks/v1/card/notification.md)
- [card.updated](https://docs.rain.xyz/changelog/webhooks/v1/card/updated.md)
- [company.updated](https://docs.rain.xyz/changelog/webhooks/v1/company/updated.md)
- [contract.created](https://docs.rain.xyz/changelog/webhooks/v1/contract/created.md)
- [dispute.chargebackCreated](https://docs.rain.xyz/changelog/webhooks/v1/dispute/chargebackCreated.md)
- [dispute.created](https://docs.rain.xyz/changelog/webhooks/v1/dispute/created.md)
- [dispute.evidenceRequested](https://docs.rain.xyz/changelog/webhooks/v1/dispute/evidenceRequested.md)
- [dispute.updated](https://docs.rain.xyz/changelog/webhooks/v1/dispute/updated.md)
- [report.created](https://docs.rain.xyz/changelog/webhooks/v1/report/created.md)
- [transaction.created](https://docs.rain.xyz/changelog/webhooks/v1/transaction/collateral/created.md)
- [transaction.completed](https://docs.rain.xyz/changelog/webhooks/v1/transaction/payment/completed.md)
- [transaction.created](https://docs.rain.xyz/changelog/webhooks/v1/transaction/payment/created.md)
- [transaction.completed](https://docs.rain.xyz/changelog/webhooks/v1/transaction/spend/completed.md)
- [transaction.created](https://docs.rain.xyz/changelog/webhooks/v1/transaction/spend/created.md)
- [transaction.requested](https://docs.rain.xyz/changelog/webhooks/v1/transaction/spend/requested.md)
- [transaction.updated](https://docs.rain.xyz/changelog/webhooks/v1/transaction/spend/updated.md)
- [transaction-transfer.completed](https://docs.rain.xyz/changelog/webhooks/v1/transactionTransfer/completed.md)
- [transaction-transfer.created](https://docs.rain.xyz/changelog/webhooks/v1/transactionTransfer/created.md)
- [transactionTransfer.updated](https://docs.rain.xyz/changelog/webhooks/v1/transactionTransfer/updated.md)
- [user.updated](https://docs.rain.xyz/changelog/webhooks/v1/user/updated.md)
- [challenge.requested](https://docs.rain.xyz/changelog/webhooks/v2/challenge/requested.md)
- [3DS Forwarding](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/3ds-forwarding.md): When a cardholder makes a transaction that requires 3D Secure authentication, Rain will send a webhook to your configured endpoint containing the challenge details. Your system should then deliver this one-time password (OTP) to the cardholder through your preferred delivery method (SMS, email, etc)…
- [API Integration & Webhooks](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/api-integration-webhooks.md): Before you go live with Rain, complete the following setup steps and review common questions about our APIs and webhooks.
- [Application States](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/application-states.md): There are several possible application statuses on the Rain platform. This page describes each one, detailing why an application might be in that state, what you should do, and what transitions to expect.
- [Authorization](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/authorization.md): Authorization occurs when a user swipes, taps, or inserts their card. The merchant estimates the cost of the goods and/or services and requests authorization for that amount. No money is moved at this stage. Instead, the card issuer places a hold on the funds to ensure they remain available.
- [Authorization, Declines & Risk Rules](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/authorization-declines-risk-rules.md): This page outlines the authorization codes, common decline reasons, and risk prevention rules enforced by Rain before and during transaction processing.
- [Partner-managed](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/authorization-partner-managed.md): In the Partner-managed authorization process, when a cardholder initiates a purchase, our system validates key criteria before sending the authorization request. These checks include verifying the card's validity, confirming the reserve balance, assessing spending limits, and applying merchant-speci…
- [Rain-managed](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/authorization-rain-managed.md): In the Rain-managed Authorization process, our system ensures that each transaction undergoes a comprehensive validation before funds are held for settlement. These validations include verifying the card's validity, checking the user's balance, assessing spending limits, and enforcing custom merchan…
- [Authorizing Transactions](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/authorizing-transactions.md)
- [report](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/bin-reports.md): Webhook triggered when processor BIN report files arrive in storage, available for BIN-Sponsorship programs.
- [Blocked MCC's and Merchants](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/blocked-mccs-and-merchants.md)
- [Bulk Shipping](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/bulk-shipping.md): Bulk shipping allows you to group multiple cards into a single shipment for efficient delivery. This reduces shipping costs and simplifies distribution, especially for organizations managing large numbers of cards.
- [card](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/card.md)
- [Card Management & Usage](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/card-management-usage.md)
- [collateral](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/collateral.md): Webhook triggered when a new collateral contract is created for an approved user in a Rain-managed program.
- [company](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/company.md)
- [Compliance](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/compliance.md): Compliance processes ensure that user verification and regulatory requirements are met. This includes tracking application statuses, submitting necessary documentation, and integrating with compliance vendors.
- [KYC/KYB Documents](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/compliance-documentation.md): When registering customers, you must submit some documents for the compliance checks. They differ depending on whether the customer is a person (KYC) or a business (KYB).
- [Decline Reasons](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/decline-reasons.md)
- [Developer Dashboard](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/developer-dashboard.md): The Developer Dashboard allows you to manage your API keys, configure webhooks, and oversee your integration with Rain.
- [disputes](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/disputes.md)
- [Escalations and Support Paths](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/escalations-and-support-paths.md): Use this guide to determine when to escalate issues to Rain Support, how to escalate and where to find reference codes for troubleshooting transactions.
- [Overview](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/faq-pre-go-live.md): KYC compliance requirements for individual and corporate applications, including required documents and information.
- [FAQs: Post-Go Live](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/faqs-post-go-live.md): This page will help you with Rain once you're live.
- [First Steps](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/first-steps.md): Before you start using the guides, it is important to identify which program type and management model applies to your implementation. This will ensure you follow the correct guides and API references.
- [Force Capture](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/force-capture.md)
- [Handling Overcapture](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/handling-overcapture.md): Permitted overcapture limits by merchant category code (MCC) and region
- [Industry Codes - KYB](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/industry-codes-kyb.md)
- [Overview](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/integration-resources.md)
- [IP Whitelisting](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/ip-whitelisting.md): When integrating with Rain's API, you may need to whitelist our IP addresses in your firewall or security settings. This is particularly important if you have strict network security policies or if you're using webhooks that only accept connections from trusted sources.
- [Issuing Cards](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/issuing-cards.md): Once a user's application is approved, you can issue them cards using the  endpoint. We support both virtual and physical cards, each with different setup requirements.
- [Ledgering Best Practices](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/ledgering-best-practices.md)
- [Ledgering System](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/ledgering-system.md): Understand how Rain tracks financial movements using double-entry accounting with balanced ledger entries across accounts and books.
- [Authorization Method Codes](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/list-of-authorizationmethod-codes.md): These  codes appear in transaction webhooks. This list corresponds to the possible code values and respective descriptions.
- [Managing a Card's PIN](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/managing-a-cards-pin.md)
- [Managing Cards](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/managing-cards.md): Once you have users in the system, you can issue and manage their cards. Every card is linked to a specific user, and you must have at least one user before issuing a card.
- [Managing Collateral](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/managing-collateral.md): Collateral management is key to ensuring users can transact securely and efficiently within the system. The type of collateral management process depends on whether the program is Rain-managed or Partner-managed.
- [Managing Subtenants](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/managing-subtenants.md)
- [Managing Users](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/managing-users.md): Learn how to add, deactivate, and delete users in your Corporate or Authorized User programs.
- [Multi-Capture Transaction](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/multi-capture-transaction.md)
- [Occupation Codes - KYC](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/occupation-codes.md)
- [Offramps (Beta)](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/offramps.md): Enable users to offramp crypto to fiat using automations with payment accounts for ACH, and wire transfers.
- [Onramps (Beta)](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/onramps.md): Enable users to onramp fiat funds into crypto using automations with virtual accounts for ACH, and wire transfers.
- [Over Capture Transaction](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/over-capture-transaction.md): Webhook examples for an over capture transaction, where the final settlement amount exceeds the original authorization.
- [Overview](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/overview.md): Explore answers to common questions about using Rain—from setup and card usage to handling transactions, disputes, and support.
- [Partial Capture Transaction](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/partial-capture-transaction.md)
- [Partner-managed Programs](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/partner-managed-programs.md): Before your customers can start spending on Rain, you'll need collateral in your Rain account. Rain deploys a collateral contract per chain for your card program which serves as the reserve balance.
- [Persona Token Sharing](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/persona-token-sharing.md): If you already use Persona for compliance, we can simplify some of our application flows by using their token sharing system. Here's how it works:
- [Physical Card Shipping](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/physical-card-shipping.md)
- [Pre-Live Setup](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/pre-live-setup.md): This page covers frequently asked questions related to environment setup, user creation, language support, and data access prior to going live with Rain.
- [Pricing and fees](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/pricing-and-fees.md)
- [Push Provisioning](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/push-provisioning.md): Enable users to add their Rain card to Apple Wallet or Google Wallet directly from your app.
- [Quickstart](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/quickstart.md): Get from zero to a funded card in production. Follow these steps to set up your Rain integration end-to-end.
- [Rain-managed Programs](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/rain-managed-programs.md): Rain-managed programs simplify collateral management by handling smart contract operations for users. Before spending on Rain, customers must deposit collateral into their assigned contracts.
- [Refund Transaction](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/refund-transaction.md)
- [Reporting Field Descriptions](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/reporting-field-descriptions.md): Understand each field in your daily tenant report CSV
- [ `SessionId` Public Keys (Development and Production)](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/resource-sessionid-keys.md)
- [Sample Webhooks](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/sample-webhooks.md): This example demonstrates a complete transaction lifecycle through webhook notifications, showing how a transaction progresses from initial authorization to final settlement with an amount adjustment.
- [Mint Test Tokens for Collateral (Sandbox)](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/sandbox-mint-test-tokens-for-collateral.md)
- [Overview](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/settlement.md): Once both the merchant and the payment network are ready, they proceed with settlement. The merchant notifies the network of the amount to be transferred, and the network settles the balance with the card issuer. The network may adjust the amount the merchant can request based on prior authorization…
- [Partner-managed](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/settlement-partner-managed.md): In the Partner-managed Settlement process, your system manages authorization holds and posts transactions after receiving settlement webhooks from Rain. Once a merchant completes a settlement, Rain sends a webhook with the final amount. Your system must then release the corresponding authorization h…
- [Rain-managed](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/settlement-rain-managed.md): In the Rain-managed Settlement process, our system handles how authorized transactions are finalized and how funds are moved. Depending on the merchant’s request, settlements can capture the full authorized amount, a partial amount, or even exceed the authorization under certain conditions. In some…
- [Sharing Data from External Compliance Vendors](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/sharing-data-from-external-compliance-vendors.md): We offer support for integrating other compliance vendors to simplify the application flow. The list of vendors we support may change over time. You can find the most up-to-date list in your developer dashboard. Here's how to get integrated.
- [Signing Up a Customer](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/signing-up-a-customer.md): Learn how to submit applications and compliance documentation to enroll customers in your issuing program.
- [Smart Contract Wallets](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/smart-contract-wallets.md)
- [Submitting Pre-Collected User Documents](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/submitting-pre-collected-user-documents.md)
- [Final Rejection](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/sumsub-final-rejection.md): Final rejections are issued when an applicant fails verification due to serious, non-correctable issues. These rejections are permanent and the applicant is not allowed to reapply. Common reasons include identity fraud, association with sanctioned individuals, or violations of regulatory requirement…
- [Overview](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/sumsub-temporary-rejection.md): Temporary rejections occur when an applicant's submission contains fixable issues. These rejections are not final and allow the applicant to correct the problems and resubmit their documents. Common reasons include low-quality photos, expired or incomplete documents, and mismatched personal data.
- [Sumsub Token Sharing](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/sumsub-token-sharing.md): If you already use Sumsub for compliance, we can simplify some of our application flows by using their  system. Here's how it works:
- [Supported Chains and Tokens](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/supported-chains-and-tokens.md): Rain is currently live on the following chains with support for the following tokens
- [Testing Transactions in Sandbox](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/testing-transactions-in-sandbox.md)
- [transaction](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/transaction.md): The webhooks covered here are triggered for all spend, collateral, payment, or fee-related transactions, notifying your systems as transactions move through their lifecycle states of requested, created, updated, and completed. These notifications allow you to integrate transaction events into your s…
- [Transaction Issues & Disputes](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/transaction-issues-disputes.md): This page covers how Rain manages unauthorized transactions, disputes, refunds, chargebacks, pending payments, in-progress authorizations, and terminal declines.
- [Transaction Lifecycle](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/transaction-lifecycle.md): Transactions typically occur in two phases:
- [Transfer Collateral Accounts](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/transfer-collateral-accounts.md): If a user needs to update their wallet address used to manage a collateral account, they can follow the process below to update the admin addresses.
- [Ultimate Beneficial Owner (UBO)](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/ultimate-beneficial-owner-ubo.md)
- [user](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/user.md)
- [User Balance](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/user-balance.md): The  API provides an overview for a specific user within a tenant. It retrieves key balance metrics, including the user's credit limit, pending charges, posted charges, balance due, and spending power. You can use these metrics for managing user accounts, tracking spending, and ensuring financial ac…
- [Using Encryption Outside of a Browser Environment](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/using-encryption-outside-of-a-browser-environment.md): Some of the code on the  guide may not work outside of a browser due to reliance on  and other browser APIs. Below are alternative implementations for server-side environments using Node.js.
- [Restrictions](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/va-automation-restrictions.md): Regional, country, and industry restrictions that apply to onramp and offramp automations.
- [Verification and Compliance](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/verification-and-compliance.md): This page answers common questions related to identity verification, user rejections, AML flags, and compliance documentation for onboarding users in Rain.
- [Verification Troubleshooting](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/verification-troubleshooting.md): Common issues and solutions for the Sumsub identity verification flow, including camera errors, iframe permissions, and SDK integration problems.
- [Viewing Encrypted Card Details](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/viewing-encrypted-card-details.md): For security and compliance reasons, full card details (card number and CVC) are encrypted and should only be accessed when necessary. While the last four digits and expiry date are always accessible, full card details require a secure retrieval process.
- [Managing Transactions](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/viewing-transactions.md): To monitor and manage transactions efficiently, you can retrieve transaction data, add memos, and attach receipts using the available API endpoints.
- [Overview](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/webhooks-overview.md): Webhooks enable real-time event notifications, allowing you to integrate external systems with Rain.
- [Overview](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/what-is-rain.md): Rain is a modern card-issuing platform that helps businesses create, manage, and scale card programs globally. As a Visa Network issuer, Rain provides the infrastructure to support multiple financial use cases, whether you're a fintech, platform, or custodian.
- [Withdraw Collateral](https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/withdraw-collateral.md): Only an admin wallet on the smart contract can withdraw collateral. However, withdrawals can be made to any address.
- [](https://docs.rain.xyz/index.md)
- [Add a new UBO to a company application](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/applications/add-a-new-ubo-to-a-company-application.md)
- [Create a consumer application for a user](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/applications/create-a-consumer-application-for-a-user.md): Creates a consumer application for a user. Can be used directly without calling the initiate endpoint first. Identity verification is handled through Sumsub share token, Persona share token, or by providing personal information through the API. The application goes through KYC review once created.
- [Create a corporate application for a company](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/applications/create-a-corporate-application-for-a-company.md)
- [Get the status of a company's corporate application](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/applications/get-the-status-of-a-companys-corporate-application.md)
- [Get the status of a company's corporate application by external id](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/applications/get-the-status-of-a-companys-corporate-application-by-external-id.md): Retrieves a company's corporate application using the tenant-defined external identifier. The externalId must have been set when creating or updating the company.
- [Get the status of a user's consumer application](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/applications/get-the-status-of-a-users-consumer-application.md)
- [Get the status of a user's consumer application by external id](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/applications/get-the-status-of-a-users-consumer-application-by-external-id.md): Retrieves a user's consumer application using the tenant-defined external identifier. The externalId must have been set when creating or updating the user.
- [Initiate a consumer application for a user](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/applications/initiate-a-consumer-application-for-a-user.md): Initiates a consumer application for a user. Use this endpoint when you plan to upload existing identity documents - it creates a user record and returns completion links for document verification. If you're not uploading documents, skip this and use the Create endpoint directly.
- [Update a company UBO's application](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/applications/update-a-company-ubos-application.md)
- [Update a company's corporate application](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/applications/update-a-companys-corporate-application.md)
- [Update a user's consumer application](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/applications/update-a-users-consumer-application.md)
- [Upload a document to support a company's corporate application](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/applications/upload-a-document-to-support-a-companys-corporate-application.md)
- [Upload a document to support a user's consumer application](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/applications/upload-a-document-to-support-a-users-consumer-application.md)
- [Upload a UBO's document to support a company's corporate application](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/applications/upload-a-ubos-document-to-support-a-companys-corporate-application.md)
- [Authenticating with the API](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/authenticating-with-the-api.md)
- [Create an automation](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/automations/create-an-automation.md): Creates an automation that automatically processes incoming funds. Automations can route funds from fiat to on-chain or from on-chain to fiat.
- [Delete an automation](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/automations/delete-an-automation.md): Deletes an automation by ID.
- [Get all automations](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/automations/get-all-automations.md): Returns all automations for the tenant, optionally filtered by team.
- [Get an automation](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/automations/get-an-automation.md): Returns a single automation by ID.
- [Get a company's credit balances](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/balances/get-a-companys-credit-balances.md)
- [Get a tenant's credit balances](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/balances/get-a-tenants-credit-balances.md)
- [Get a users's credit balances](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/balances/get-a-userss-credit-balances.md)
- [Create a card for a user](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/cards/create-a-card-for-a-user.md): Creates a card for a user. For physical cards, the user's first name and last name must contain only latin characters (A-Z, a-z, spaces and hyphens) without accent marks.
- [Get a card by its id](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/cards/get-a-card-by-its-id.md)
- [Get a card's encrypted data](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/cards/get-a-cards-encrypted-data.md)
- [Get a card's PIN](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/cards/get-a-cards-pin.md)
- [Get all the cards for a user or company](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/cards/get-all-the-cards-for-a-user-or-company.md)
- [Get processor details of a card](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/cards/get-processor-details-of-a-card.md): Retrieve the processor card ID for a specific card.
- [Replace a virtual card](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/cards/replace-a-virtual-card.md): Replaces a virtual card with a new one. The old card is canceled and a new card is created with a new PAN, token, and expiration date. The new card inherits the spending limits from the old card. Physical cards cannot be replaced.
- [Update a card](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/cards/update-a-card.md)
- [Update a card's PIN.](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/cards/update-a-cards-pin.md)
- [Charge a company a custom fee](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/companies/charge-a-company-a-custom-fee.md)
- [Get a company by external id](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/companies/get-a-company-by-external-id.md): Retrieves a company using the tenant-defined external identifier. The externalId must have been set when creating or updating the company.
- [Get a company by id](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/companies/get-a-company-by-id.md)
- [Get all companies](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/companies/get-all-companies.md)
- [Update a company](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/companies/update-a-company.md)
- [Create a smart contract for a company](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/contracts/create-a-smart-contract-for-a-company.md)
- [Create a smart contract for a user](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/contracts/create-a-smart-contract-for-a-user.md)
- [Get smart contract information for a company](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/contracts/get-smart-contract-information-for-a-company.md)
- [Get smart contract information for a user](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/contracts/get-smart-contract-information-for-a-user.md)
- [Update a smart contract](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/contracts/update-a-smart-contract.md)
- [Create a dispute for a transaction](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/disputes/create-a-dispute-for-a-transaction.md)
- [Delete a dispute attachment](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/disputes/delete-a-dispute-attachment.md): Delete a specific attachment from a dispute.
- [Download a dispute attachment](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/disputes/download-a-dispute-attachment.md): Download a specific attachment file. For disputes with legacy evidence, use "legacy" as the attachmentId.
- [Get a dispute by its id](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/disputes/get-a-dispute-by-its-id.md)
- [Get a dispute's file evidence](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/disputes/get-a-disputes-file-evidence.md)
- [Get all disputes](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/disputes/get-all-disputes.md)
- [List a dispute's attachments](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/disputes/list-a-disputes-attachments.md): Returns all attachments for a dispute. For disputes that only have legacy evidence (uploaded before multi-attachment support), a single virtual attachment with ID "legacy" is returned.
- [Update a dispute](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/disputes/update-a-dispute.md)
- [Upload a file as evidence for a dispute](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/disputes/upload-a-file-as-evidence-for-a-dispute.md)
- [Upload an attachment for a dispute](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/disputes/upload-an-attachment-for-a-dispute.md): Upload a file as evidence for a dispute. Maximum file size is 20MB. Each dispute can have up to 10 attachments.
- [Idempotency for APIs](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/idempotency.md)
- [Create a key](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/keys/create-a-key.md)
- [Delete a key](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/keys/delete-a-key.md)
- [Create a payment account](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/paymentaccounts/create-a-payment-account.md): Creates a payment account that can be used as a destination for automations. Supports external fiat accounts.
- [Delete a payment account](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/paymentaccounts/delete-a-payment-account.md): Deletes a payment account by ID.
- [Get a payment account](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/paymentaccounts/get-a-payment-account.md): Returns a single payment account by ID.
- [Get all payment accounts](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/paymentaccounts/get-all-payment-accounts.md): Returns all payment accounts for the tenant, optionally filtered by team.
- [Initiate a payment for a company](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/payments/initiate-a-payment-for-a-company.md)
- [Initiate a payment for a user](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/payments/initiate-a-payment-for-a-user.md)
- [Initiate a payment for an authorized user tenant](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/payments/initiate-a-payment-for-an-authorized-user-tenant.md)
- [Create a quote](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/quotes/create-a-quote.md): Creates a quote for a transfer with an exchange rate and fee breakdown. Quotes expire after a short period (typically 15 minutes).
- [Rain API](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/rain-api.md)
- [Get a tenant's monthly report](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/reports/get-a-tenants-monthly-report.md): Retrieves the monthly report for the specified year and month
- [Get a tenant's report](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/reports/get-a-tenants-report.md)
- [Create a bulk shipping group](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/shipping/create-a-bulk-shipping-group.md)
- [Get a bulk shipping group](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/shipping/get-a-bulk-shipping-group.md)
- [Get all bulk shipping groups](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/shipping/get-all-bulk-shipping-groups.md)
- [Validate an address for shipping](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/shipping/validate-an-address-for-shipping.md): Validates a shipping address using Google Address Validation API with fallback to Google Geocoding API. Returns validation result with confidence level.
- [Get payment signature for a company](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/signatures/get-payment-signature-for-a-company.md)
- [Get payment signature for a user](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/signatures/get-payment-signature-for-a-user.md)
- [Get payment signature for an authorized user tenant](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/signatures/get-payment-signature-for-an-authorized-user-tenant.md)
- [Get withdrawal signature for a company](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/signatures/get-withdrawal-signature-for-a-company.md)
- [Get withdrawal signature for a user](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/signatures/get-withdrawal-signature-for-a-user.md)
- [Get withdrawal signature for an authorized user tenant](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/signatures/get-withdrawal-signature-for-an-authorized-user-tenant.md)
- [Create a subtenant](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/subtenants/create-a-subtenant.md)
- [Get a subtenant by its id](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/subtenants/get-a-subtenant-by-its-id.md)
- [Get all subtenants](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/subtenants/get-all-subtenants.md)
- [Update a subtenant](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/subtenants/update-a-subtenant.md)
- [Get a transaction by its id](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/transactions/get-a-transaction-by-its-id.md)
- [Get a transaction's receipt](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/transactions/get-a-transactions-receipt.md)
- [Get all transactions](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/transactions/get-all-transactions.md)
- [Update a transaction](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/transactions/update-a-transaction.md)
- [Upload a transaction's receipt](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/transactions/upload-a-transactions-receipt.md)
- [Create a transfer](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/transfers/create-a-transfer.md): Creates a transfer from a quote. The response includes a deposit address where the user should send crypto. The transfer expires if funds are not deposited before the expiration time.
- [Charge a user a custom fee](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/users/charge-a-user-a-custom-fee.md)
- [Create a user in a company](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/users/create-a-user-in-a-company.md)
- [Delete a user](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/users/delete-a-user.md)
- [Get a user by external id](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/users/get-a-user-by-external-id.md): Retrieves a user using the tenant-defined external identifier. The externalId must have been set when creating or updating the user.
- [Get a user by id](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/users/get-a-user-by-id.md)
- [Get all users](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/users/get-all-users.md)
- [Update a user](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/users/update-a-user.md)
- [Create a secondary signing API key for webhook signatures](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/webhooks/create-a-secondary-signing-api-key-for-webhook-signatures.md): Creates a new secondary signing API key that can be used to sign webhook requests. This endpoint is used for API key rotation - the secondary key will be used alongside the primary key to sign webhooks, allowing you to update your systems before promoting the secondary key to primary.
- [Get a webhook by its id](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/webhooks/get-a-webhook-by-its-id.md)
- [Get all webhooks](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/webhooks/get-all-webhooks.md)
- [Get webhook configuration](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/webhooks/get-webhook-configuration.md): Retrieves the current webhook configuration for the tenant. The configuration controls webhook behavior using pattern-based rules that match webhook event types (e.g., 'card.*', 'transaction.created.*'). Each pattern can specify a version.
- [Patch webhook configuration](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/webhooks/patch-webhook-configuration.md): Partially updates the webhook configuration for the tenant. Only the provided patterns will be updated or removed. Set a pattern's value to null to remove it from the configuration. Existing patterns not included in the request body will remain unchanged.
- [Promote the secondary signing API key to primary](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/webhooks/promote-the-secondary-signing-api-key-to-primary.md): Promotes the secondary signing API key to become the new primary signing API key. The old primary key will be deleted. This completes the API key rotation process.
- [Update webhook configuration](https://docs.rain.xyz/reference/webhooks/update-webhook-configuration.md): Replaces the entire webhook configuration for the tenant. The configuration uses pattern-based rules to control webhook behavior. Patterns can use wildcards (e.g., 'card.*' matches all card events, 'transaction.created.*' matches all transaction created events). Each pattern can specify a version.
- [Card Issuance](https://docs.rain.xyz/sdks/card-issuance.md): Issue a virtual or physical card using the Rain SDK, from application submission through approval to card creation.
- [Balances & Transactions](https://docs.rain.xyz/sdks/client-sdk-balances-and-transactions.md): Query native and ERC-20 token balances, fetch transaction history, send tokens, and estimate gas fees using the Rain Client SDK.
- [Error Reference](https://docs.rain.xyz/sdks/client-sdk-error-reference.md): Complete reference for Rain Client SDK error codes on Android and iOS, with handling patterns and troubleshooting guidance.
- [Client SDK Overview](https://docs.rain.xyz/sdks/client-sdk-overview.md): Build withdrawal transactions, query balances, send tokens, and interact with Rain collateral contracts from your Android or iOS app.
- [Withdraw Collateral](https://docs.rain.xyz/sdks/client-sdk-withdraw-collateral.md): Build and execute collateral withdrawals from your Android or iOS app using the Rain Client SDK. Construct EIP-712 messages, encode withdrawal calldata, and submit transactions with any wallet provider.
- [Configuration](https://docs.rain.xyz/sdks/configuration.md): Configure error handling, retries, timeouts, pagination, and logging for the Rain SDK.
- [Funding & Transfers](https://docs.rain.xyz/sdks/funding-and-transfers.md): Deposit collateral, process withdrawals, and check balances using the Rain SDK, for both Rain-managed and Partner-managed programs.
- [SDK Overview](https://docs.rain.xyz/sdks/overview.md): Install the Rain SDK, authenticate with your API key, and make your first API call in TypeScript or Python.
- [Transaction Monitoring](https://docs.rain.xyz/sdks/transaction-monitoring.md): List, filter, and manage transactions using the Rain SDK. Build dashboards and reconciliation flows with typed transaction data.

## OpenAPI Specs

- [openapi](https://docs.rain.xyz/openapi.json)
